initial_prompt:
Super-Eywa v1 runtime prompt snapshot.
Turn index: 1
Node depth: 0
Allowed decisions: ['transmute']
Prompt family: transmute
Base header prompt: ''
Selected prompt text:
Reformulate the problem to include a verification phase based on limiting cases and dimensional consistency. Instruct the agent to: 1. Define the functional forms for each component of the ratio. 2. Verify these forms by checking their behavior in extreme limits (e.g., high-acceleration vs. low-acceleration regimes) and ensuring dimensional homogeneity. 3. Perform the algebraic simplification of the ratio only after the component forms have been validated against these physical constraints. This ensures the derivation is built upon physically plausible foundations.

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "transmute",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "message_for_next_agent": "plain-text reformulation for the next agent"
}
Additional instruction prompt profiles: []
System prompt:

Question:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: atlas-derived-A2-unruh-de-sitter-temperature-ratio-mond-interpolation
Title: A2. Unruh-de Sitter Temperature Ratio = MOND Interpolation

Problem:
What is the ratio T_U(a)/T_dS(a) of flat-space Unruh temperature to de Sitter-modified Unruh temperature?

Grading target:
Exact algebraic identity match plus numerical fit verification.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
